feed.title: SAP Community - Cloud Integration
feed.subtitle: Cloud Integration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: BP_DATAREPL_FND/006 error when replicating Business Partner from SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 to SAP S/
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am working on the integration scenario 6KO - Opportunity to Order with SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 and I am facing an issue with Business Partner replication from SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 back to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Scenario:
SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 → SAP Cloud Integration → SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Issue:
When a Business Partner is modified in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 and the replication is triggered back to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the Message Monitoring in S/4HANA shows the error:

BP_DATAREPL_FND / 006

What is already configured:

  • Communication System created in S/4HANA with Business System field filled with the Display ID obtained from the default communication system in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2
  • Communication Arrangement SAP_COM_0008 active with the correct Communication System assigned
  • Additional Properties: Integration with C4C enabled = X and No Multiple Address Handling = X
  • Inbound Service active: Business Partner - Replicate from Client to SAP S/4HANA Cloud (SOAP)
  • Communication User linked to the Communication System and Communication Arrangement
  • iFlow Replicate Business Partner to SAP S4HANA Cloud deployed and running in SAP Cloud Integration

Question:
What could be causing the error BP_DATAREPL_FND/006 with all this configuration in place? Is there any additional configuration needed specifically for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to authorize the inbound replication coming from SAP Sales Cloud Version 2?

Any help or relevant SAP Notes would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: SAP CPI OData V4 POST – Invalid CSRF Token Error for WarehouseTask Confirm API (EWM A2X)
entry.content:
Hi,

I am currently working with the standard SAP EWM API – Warehouse Order and Task (A2X), specifically using the WarehouseTask entity. This is an OData V4 API.

🔹 Scenario

I am trying to perform a POST operation to confirm a Warehouse Task using the below endpoint:

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_warehouse_order_task_2/srvd_a2x/sap/warehouseorder/0001/WarehouseTask/EU20/1234563436/0/SAP__self.ConfirmWarehouseTaskHndlgUnit

Where:

  • EU20 → EWM Warehouse
  • 1234563436 → Warehouse Task
  • 0 → Warehouse Task Item

🔹 Approach Followed

  1. First, I executed a GET call on the same API to:

    • Fetch the CSRF token
    • Retrieve the corresponding ETag for the specific combination (Warehouse, WarehouseTask, WarehouseTaskItem)in the body.

  2. For the POST request:

    • I used the same CSRF token in the header
    • Set the header:
      If-Match = <ETag (from GET response)>

🔹 Issue Faced

While triggering the POST request, I am receiving the following error:

Invalid CSRF token

🔹 Observed Behavior

  • In the CPI trace logs (receiver side), I can see that the CSRF token is being passed in the request header.
  • However, after the failure:
    • In the request headers attachment, the CSRF token appears as "fetch" instead of the actual token value.

This is creating a confusing situation, as the token seems to be passed but still results in an "Invalid CSRF token" error.

🔹 API Reference

For reference, I am using the API from SAP Business Accelerator Hub:
Warehouse Task Confirm API

🔹 Ask

Has anyone encountered a similar issue while working with this API or OData V4 CSRF handling in CPI?

Any guidance on:

  • Correct CSRF handling approach, or
  • Possible reasons why the token is getting replaced with "fetch"
    would be really helpful.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Define bank transfer tile App id: F3759 - Custom Field are not available in Adapt UI
entry.content: Custom field was created on "Custom Field app " using Business Context " Payment Request " and "Bank Account Master Data ", but when we are trying to add custom field on "Define bank transfer tile App id: F3759" using Adaption UI , new custom field is not available to add on app

entry.title: Solution for Attachment Replication from SAP Business Suite to sales cloud V2?
entry.content:
The standard Replicate Sales Order and Sales Quote from SAP S4HANA iFlow only covers header, products, prices, and parties "no attachments".

Is there a standard SAP iFlow/content package for replicating S/4 sales order/quote attachments to Sales Cloud V2 (similar to the old C4C AttachmentFolderReplicationRequest_Out scenario)? Or is a custom post-exit using API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV → V2 AttachmentFolder the only way? or there is any other way to handle this integration

Has anyone built this in a live project?

Many Thanks,

Ruchita

entry.title: Issue with oauth authentication while calling Integration flow
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I have created one Oauth clinent and also added the role ESBMesseging.send role to it, i can fetch the access token but not able to call the endpoint iflow of https in CPI and getting 403 error

entry.title: API is still valid ?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We were using the API to extract sender host type and Receiver host type .Just wanted to know if this API "{cpi_url}/api/v1/IntegrationConnections?$expand=IntegrationFlows&$filter=ResolvedConnection eq true&$inlinecount=allpages  " been retired ?  If so, Any new API been released or any API available which provide the same details.

Below is the blog from where we picked the API .

SAP Cloud Platform Integration - Connection count ... - SAP Community

entry.title: Not able to create story report
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

When trying to create story report in SF QAS instance, it is giving a blank page and giving message - "Full Screen mode toggled as we detected you are using a low resolution. More information about our minimum resolution requirements can be found at help.sap.com " 

Kindly find the attached screenshot with the error.

entry.title: SAP BTP Neo to Cloud Foundry migration using Postman collection
entry.content:
Hi everyone.

I am performing SAP BTP Neo to Cloud Foundry Migration using Postman Collection provided by SAP.
I am following the steps mentioned in the following SAP Help document :  'https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration-migration-guide/migrating-cloud-integration-from-neo-environment-to-multi-cloud-foundation'.
The collection 'Connect to Tenants' executed successfully. The collection 'Custom Content' executed successfully. I am able to migrate Package successfully.

Regarding uploading 'sap_cloudintegrationcertificate' from both environments, I have followed instructions as provided in SAP Help document

When executing the collection 'CPI MIG090 Security Artifacts' got the following exception for all the requests in the collection - 
'Exception: com.sap.it.cm.transport.exception.TransportManagementBackendException: No DNS name found in the system property or user provided certificate'.

( For 'sap_cloudintegrationcertificate' in Neo environment there is 'Subject Alternative Name' which contains 'DNS' value where as it is missing in Cloud Foundry environment. )

Could anyone please provide guidance on the issue?

Thank you.

entry.title: Custom Domain on Cloud Foundry subaccount to call Integration Suite endpoint on Neo subaccount
entry.content:
Dear gurus,

We are implementing FatturaPA (Italy's tax authority eDocument requirement) for a customer running Integration Suite on a Neo subaccount. Customers must register they web service endpoint under a domain thy own (see https://me.sap.com/notes/2583309 and https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/fatturapa-edocument-italy-signing-custom-domain/qaq-p/731320).

We have performed the set up in customers running both integration suite and custom domain on Neo or with both running in cloud foundry but SAP refuses to sell customer domain on Neo because it is on sunset.

Customer is migrating from Neo to Cloud Foundry as part of a large scale revamp project starting in January 2027 so migrating Integration Suite to Cloud Foundry now is out of question but FatturaPA mus be set up by October.

Can a custom domain running in a Cloud Foundry subaccount be used to call an Integration Suite endpoint running in Neo on a different subaccount?

If yes, can you kindly let us know how or point the relevant documentation? We have already tried Routes and Sharing Reserved Domains with no luck.

If no, is there any official SAP source, such a SAP Note that says so explicitly?

Thanks in advance.

Best regards,

Jose

entry.title: Electronic Invoicing France
entry.content:
Good morning,

we are implementing incoming electronic invoicing for France and we would need a sample XML file related to a French electronic invoice.

Unfortunately, we have not been able to find an example either online or within the Build Work Zone dedicated to France.

Could you please provide us with a sample XML file to be used as a reference for our implementation activities?

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite - MCP Capability - API Management - When available?
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'd like to start using the MCP capability of integration suite (see the video: https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/integration-suite/agentic-ai.html ), but I wonder when it will be available on our Standard Integration Suite tenants. The A2A will take some time so the MCP is the best guess, I guess...

Any (official) idea?

All the best

Laurens

entry.title: GitHub Integration is not available for tenants
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I understood that SAP released GitHub Integration for Integration suite. I am trying to see the GitHub related options in my tenants but cant find. 

I have admin and developer role in all tenants but I am not able to see it. 

Do we have any prerequisites to enable GitHub Inytegration?

entry.title: unable to deploy the Value mapping Iflow
entry.content:
In QA environment, we are unable to deploy the Value mapping Iflow 
'Value mappings for the employee data integration' deployment failed.
Error Details

[CONTENT][CONTENT_DEPLOY][ArtifactDeploymentFailed] : All attempts of deployment of artifact failed. Error details: Optional[Caused By : Activator start error in bundle SAP_SuccessFactors_Employee_Central_to_WorkForce_Software_Suite_Value_Mappings.DR [1051]. | Caused By : This Value Mapping contains a group () that has an Agency Identifier pair [WFS, WFS Status Ranking] : [EC, EC Employment Status], which conflicts with group

entry.title: Need to create a subaccount in Cloud Connector for Integration Suite PRD
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Create a sub account in Cloud Connector Cloud Connector

Attached details of BTP Integration Suite PRD details as attached. Let me know what the process and time would be taken for subaccount creation.

entry.title: When will the SAP Integration Suite Certification be active again?
entry.content:
So, the SAP Certified - Integration Developer certification is currently under maintenance, I wanted to schedule a slot for the certification today but I don't seem to be able to do it

 Any idea by what time will this certification be active again as I am in a bit of a time crunch with regard to this cert...

Thanks

entry.title: How to Implement a Queuing System in SAP Integration Suite, Starter Edition?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently using SAP Integration Suite, Starter Edition, with Cloud Integration capacity.

As part of our integration project, we need to implement a queuing mechanism to manage a high volume of incoming requests and process them reliably without overloading the integration flow or the target SAP system.

We would like your recommendations regarding the following points:

  • Is it possible to implement a queuing system with the Starter Edition?
  • Does this edition support JMS queues, or is an additional capability or license required?
  • If JMS is unavailable, what alternative architecture would you recommend?
  • Can Data Store operations be used to create a reliable queuing mechanism?
  • How can we control parallel processing, retries, message order, and error handling?
  • Are there any known capacity or performance limitations in the Starter Edition?

Our objective is to receive requests from an external system, store them temporarily, and process them asynchronously according to the available capacity.

Any recommended architecture, configuration example, or relevant documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Cloud Foundry not accesible/visible under the sub-account in SAP BTP cockpit
entry.content:
HI All,

I have created recently the Subaccount in SAP Trial but it I am not able to see any cloud foundry tab in the left penal. not sure its a issue in BTP recently, can you please help.

 

Regards,

Dibyajyoti Nanda

entry.title: handshake_failure
entry.content:
I need to create a integration to retrieve data about in employee using rest API developed by Brazil government.

 

The endpoint is this : https://papiecotrab.dataprev.gov.br/credito-trabalhador-empregador/v1.0.0/dados-consignacoes-empregador

 

I need to develop this integration in SAP Cloud Integration, but when I try to test the connectivity, I received this error:

 

“javax.net.ssl.SSLHandshakeException: (handshake_failure) Received fatal alert: handshake_failure”

entry.title: Ereporting France implementation without einvoicing is it possible
entry.content:
hello

is it possible to implemente the Ereporting France Sales and purchasing without the full implmentation of einvoicing?
We just need the ereporting process

Thanks

entry.title: Integrating GitHub with SAP Integration Suite: A Complete Guide to Version Control and DevOps
entry.content:
Connect GitHub with SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration)

Introduction

In today's integration landscape, organizations are increasingly adopting DevOps practices and CI/CD pipelines to accelerate software delivery while maintaining governance and quality. One of the key enablers of DevOps is a robust Version Control System that allows teams to collaborate efficiently, track changes, and manage application lifecycle effectively.

SAP Integration Suite, Cloud Integration (formerly known as SAP CPI), provides native integration with Git-based repositories such as GitHub. This capability allows integration developers to store, version, and manage integration artifacts directly in a Git repository, bringing modern source control practices into the integration development lifecycle.

By integrating GitHub with SAP Integration Suite, organizations can:

  • Maintain version history of integration artifacts.

  • Enable collaboration among multiple developers.

  • Support branching and release management strategies.

  • Implement CI/CD pipelines.

  • Maintain backup and recovery mechanisms.

  • Improve governance and auditability of changes.

In this blog, we will walk through the complete process of:

  • Creating a GitHub Personal Access Token.

  • Creating and configuring a GitHub repository.

  • Establishing connectivity between SAP Integration Suite and GitHub.

  • Performing Git operations such as Push, Pull, and Import.

  • Understanding best practices for managing integration artifacts using Git.

Why Use GitHub with SAP Integration Suite?

Traditionally, integration artifacts were managed directly within SAP Integration Suite, making collaboration and version management challenging in larger projects.

Git integration solves these challenges by introducing capabilities such as:

Feature Benefit

Version ControlTrack every modification to integration artifacts
CollaborationMultiple developers can work simultaneously
Backup & RecoveryRepository acts as a central backup
GovernanceBetter control using branching strategies
AuditabilityComplete history of changes
CI/CD IntegrationEnables automated deployment pipelines

Git integration becomes extremely useful in enterprise projects where multiple developers work across various environments such as DEV, QA, UAT, and PROD.

Architecture Overview

SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration)
                |
                | HTTPS + Personal Access Token
                |
           GitHub Repository
                |
      ----------------------
      |         |          |
    main      develop    feature/*

Integration artifacts such as Integration Flows, Script Collections, Value Mappings, and Message Mappings are stored inside Git repositories and can be synchronized between SAP Integration Suite and GitHub.

Prerequisites

Before proceeding, ensure the following prerequisites are met.

SAP Prerequisites

  • Access to SAP Integration Suite – Cloud Integration.

  • User must have the PI_Administrator role assigned.

  • Appropriate authorizations to create and modify integration artifacts.

GitHub Prerequisites

  • A valid GitHub account.

  • Internet connectivity to access GitHub.

  • Permission to create repositories and generate Personal Access Tokens.

Step 1 – Create a Personal Access Token in GitHub

SAP Integration Suite communicates with GitHub using Git APIs. Since GitHub no longer supports password-based authentication for Git operations, authentication must be performed using a Personal Access Token (PAT).

The Personal Access Token acts as a secure credential that authorizes SAP Integration Suite to perform operations such as:

  • Read repository information

  • Push integration artifacts

  • Pull latest changes

  • Import artifacts from repositories

Navigate to GitHub Settings

Login to GitHub and navigate to:

Profile → Settings

 

Open Developer Settings

Navigate to:

Settings → Developer Settings

 

Create a Personal Access Token

Navigate to:

Personal Access Tokens → Tokens (Classic)

Click:

Generate New Token (Classic)

 

Configure the Token

Provide:

  • Token Name

  • Expiration Date

  • Required Permissions (Scopes)

For testing purposes, all repository-related scopes can be selected. However, in productive environments, it is recommended to grant only the minimum permissions required by following the Principle of Least Privilege (PoLP).

Typical scopes include:

  • repo

  • workflow

  • read:org

  • Etc

 

 

Important Note

The generated token is displayed only once by GitHub.

Ensure that you copy and securely store the token because it cannot be viewed again once the page is closed.

This token will be used while configuring Git access in SAP Integration Suite.

Step 2 – Create a Repository

A Git repository acts as the central storage location for all integration artifacts.

Every time an artifact is pushed from SAP Integration Suite, GitHub stores:

  • Source code

  • Version history

  • Commit information

  • Metadata

  • Change logs

This repository can then be used for:

  • Collaboration

  • Backup

  • Recovery

  • Audit purposes

  • CI/CD integration

Navigate to the GitHub home page and click:

New Repository

Provide:

  • Repository Name

  • Description

  • Visibility (Public or Private)

For enterprise projects, it is recommended to use Private repositories since integration artifacts may contain business logic and sensitive information.

Example:

SAP-CPI-Integration-Artifacts

Step 3 – Create a Branch

Git uses branches to isolate development activities.

Since SAP Integration Suite performs operations against a particular branch, at least one branch must exist.

The easiest way to create the default branch is:

  1. Create a README file.

  2. Commit the file.

GitHub automatically creates the main branch.

 

Why Branching is Important

A proper branching strategy enables:

  • Parallel development

  • Controlled releases

  • Easier rollback

  • Better governance

  • Reduced conflicts

A typical enterprise branching strategy looks like:

Branch Purpose

mainProduction-ready artifacts
developIntegration testing
feature/*Development activities
releasePre-production validation
hotfixProduction bug fixes

GitHub Setup Completed

At this stage, we have successfully completed all the necessary configurations on GitHub.

The next step is to establish connectivity between GitHub and SAP Integration Suite.

Step 4 – Configure Git Access in SAP Integration Suite

Navigate to:

Settings → Git Repositories

SAP Integration Suite provides a centralized location for managing Git repository connections.

As of now, SAP Integration Suite allows configuring up to 50 Git repository connections.

This capability is useful when organizations maintain:

  • Project-specific repositories

  • Shared repositories

  • Customer-specific repositories

  • Environment-specific repositories

Click:

Edit → Add

Provide:

Field Value

Repository URLGitHub Repository URL
Access TokenGitHub Personal Access Token

Click:

Add → Save

Once saved, SAP Integration Suite validates the credentials and establishes a secure connection with GitHub.

 

Git Operations in SAP Integration Suite

After the repository connection is established, SAP Integration Suite supports three major Git operations:

  1. Git Push

  2. Git Pull

  3. Git Import

Git Push

The Git Push operation uploads integration artifacts from SAP Integration Suite into GitHub.

This operation is generally performed when:

  • A new artifact is created.

  • Existing artifacts are modified.

  • Development work is completed.

  • Changes need to be backed up.

Navigate to:

Actions → Git Push

Provide:

  • Access Token

  • Repository

  • Branch

  • Commit Message

 

 

Example commit messages:

Initial version of Customer Integration Interface
Added retry mechanism for SFTP processing
Enhanced mapping for Sales Order Interface

Benefits of Git Push

  • Creates version history.

  • Enables collaboration.

  • Maintains backup.

  • Supports CI/CD pipelines.

  • Improves governance.

After a successful push, Git information becomes visible in the integration artifact.

 

Git Pull

Git Pull is used to synchronize the latest version of the artifact from GitHub into SAP Integration Suite.

This is useful when:

  • Another developer has modified the artifact.

  • Changes have been merged into the repository.

  • Local artifacts need to be updated.

Navigate to:

Actions → Git Pull

Provide:

  • Access Token

  • Repository

  • Branch

 

Important Consideration

Pull operations can fail due to version conflicts.

For example:

  • Local artifact is already modified.

  • Repository version is different.

  • Local version is ahead of repository version.

Therefore, it is recommended to perform Pull operations regularly before starting development.

Git Import

Git Import allows developers to import artifacts directly from Git repositories into another package or tenant.

This feature is extremely useful in scenarios such as:

Tenant Migration

Moving integration artifacts between tenants.

Reusability

Importing common frameworks and templates.

Project Onboarding

New team members can quickly access existing interfaces.

Disaster Recovery

Rebuilding an Integration Suite tenant from repository content.

Navigate to:

Actions → Git Import

Provide:

  • Access Token

  • Repository

  • Branch

SAP Integration Suite displays all available artifacts in the selected branch.

Choose:

  • Add

  • Add and Open in Editor

depending on your requirement.

 

 

Recommended Git Strategy for SAP Integration Suite

A recommended branching model is:

main      → Production
release   → UAT
develop   → SIT
feature/* → Development

This approach provides:

  • Better governance

  • Parallel development

  • Easier rollback

  • Controlled deployments

  • Reduced production risks

Best Practices

Use Feature Branches

Avoid developing directly on the main branch.

Commit Frequently

Small commits are easier to track and troubleshoot.

Write Meaningful Commit Messages

Commit messages should explain the technical or business change.

Push Before Deployment

Always push the latest changes to Git before deploying to higher environments.

Protect Main Branch

Allow deployments to production only through approved pull requests and code reviews.

Use Private Repositories

Integration artifacts may contain sensitive business logic and should be protected appropriately.

Conclusion

GitHub integration with SAP Integration Suite introduces modern version control capabilities into the integration development lifecycle. By leveraging Git operations such as Push, Pull, and Import, organizations can establish a robust DevOps process, improve collaboration among developers, and maintain better governance over integration artifacts.

Integrating SAP Integration Suite with GitHub is therefore a highly recommended practice for organizations aiming to build scalable, maintainable, and enterprise-ready integration solutions while embracing modern software delivery practices.

Special thanks to @ShraddhaDas151  for the continuous support, guidance, and valuable insights that contributed to the preparation of this blog.

Thank you for reading this blog. Feel free to share your feedback, suggestions, or questions in the comments section below. Your inputs are highly appreciated and contribute to continuous learning within the SAP Community. Happy integrating!
